message:
refactor(ui): consolidate reduced motion preference into shared hook

Both AnimatedCounter and AnimatedElement components implemented their
own inline logic for detecting the user's prefers-reduced-motion
preference. This led to duplicated code and inconsistent patterns
across animation components.

Changes:
- animated-counter.tsx: Import and use useReducedMotion() hook
  - Immediately set final value when reduced motion is preferred
  - Skip animation loop entirely for better performance
  - Added prefersReducedMotion to useEffect dependency array

- animated-element.tsx: Replace inline implementation with hook
  - Removed useState + useEffect for media query handling
  - Removed MediaQueryListEvent handler registration
  - Single line import replaces 10+ lines of boilerplate

The useReducedMotion() hook from @/lib/animations/hooks provides:
- Consistent media query handling across all animation components
- Proper cleanup of event listeners
- SSR-safe implementation with appropriate initial state
- Single source of truth for accessibility preference

This refactor improves code maintainability and ensures that any
future improvements to reduced motion handling (like caching the
media query result) automatically benefit all animation components.
